markdown:
[Kyle Wiggers](https://venturebeat.com/author/kylewiggers)
September 23, 2019


![](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Folk2kzr7lpbpdtkx.public.blob.vercel-storage.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2019%2F09%2FUntitled-1-e1569252928960.png%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=85)

[Add to Google Preferred Source](https://www.google.com/preferences/source?q=venturebeat.com "Add to Google Preferred Source")

As part of a continued mission to make its Alexa intelligent assistant more accessible for everyone, Amazon today [introduced](https://blog.aboutamazon.com/devices/alexa-what-am-i-holding) a new feature called Show and Tell, which helps blind and low vision customers with an Echo Show smart display identify common pantry goods (like canned or boxed foods) that can be difficult to distinguish by touch.

Show and Tell is available starting today for Alexa customers in the U.S. on first and second-generation Echo Show devices. To get started, say "Alexa, what am I holding?" or "Alexa, what's in my hand?," which will kick off verbal and audio cues that guide you to place the item you'd like to identify in front of the Echo Show's camera.

“The whole idea for Show and Tell came about from feedback from blind and low vision customers,” said head of Amazon's Alexa for Everyone team Sarah Caplener in a statement. “We heard that product identification can be a challenge and something customers wanted Alexa’s help with. Whether a customer is sorting through a bag of groceries, or trying to determine what item was left out on the counter, we want to make those moments simpler by helping identify these items and giving customers the information they need in that moment.”

Amazon says the feature was developed in close collaboration with blind Amazon employees, and that the development team gathered input and feedback from blind and low vision customers. Additionally, they worked with the Vista Center for the Blind in Santa Cruz and other organizations throughout the process.

"I get to help create products like Show and Tell, make our Fire tablets and TVs accessible and delightful, and constantly help to imagine new ways for our products and services to improve the lives of our customers, including those with disabilities," said principal accessibility researcher Josh Miele, who's blind. He cited statistics from the World Health Organization estimating that 1.3 billion people live with some form of vision impairment, and that 15% of the population live with some sort of disability.

The debut of Show and Tell follows on the heels of accommodating features like an [adjustable speech rate](https://venturebeat.com/2019/08/07/you-can-now-adjust-how-quickly-alexa-speaks-in-the-u-s/) and Tap to Alexa, which lets [Echo Show](https://venturebeat.com/2018/07/23/amazon-makes-echo-show-more-accessible-with-tap-to-alexa/) owners access weather forecasts, news headlines, timers, and more by tapping rearrangeable shortcuts on the Show’s screen. Amazon Captioning, which launched in the U.S. last year and internationally more recently, provides captions for Alexa’s responses on Echo devices with a screen and transcribes incoming voice messages.

Amazon isn’t the only tech giant investing in accessibility, of course.

Google unveiled [three](https://venturebeat.com/2019/05/07/google-ai-accessibility-project-euphonia-diva-live-relay/) separate accessibility efforts targeting users with speech impairments, deafness, and limited mobility at its I/O 2019 developer conference earlier this year, and in July it detailed an open source AI tool, [Parrotron](https://venturebeat.com/2019/07/17/googles-parratron-is-an-ai-tool-for-people-with-speech-impediments/), that aims to help those with atypical speech become better understood by voice assistants. Meanwhile, last September saw the unveiling of [Microsoft's Soundscape](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/product/soundscape/), a navigation app that uses binaural audio -- sound recorded with two microphones arranged to create a 3D stereo sound sensation -- to help visually impaired users build mental maps and make personal route choices in unfamiliar spaces.

## More

[![nuneybits Vector art of a sleek bullet train bursting from a cl 32a805aa-272c-4b34-ac16-cf20508b7ff4](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F5RLyQIpBeVXxv0RpcXZxWQ%2Ffd9680c6d82acd8208ac341fc575f5fb%2Fnuneybits_Vector_art_of_a_sleek_bullet_train_bursting_from_a_cl_32a805aa-272c-4b34-ac16-cf20508b7ff4.webp%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
Credit: VentureBeat made with Midjourney](https://venturebeat.com/infrastructure/railway-secures-usd100-million-to-challenge-aws-with-ai-native-cloud)

"As AI models get better at writing code, more and more people are asking the age-old question: where, and how, do I run my applications?" said Jake Cooper, Railway's 28-year-old founder and chief executive, in an exclusive interview with VentureBeat. "The last generation of cloud primitives were slow and outdated, and now with AI moving everything faster, teams simply can't keep up."

[Michael Nuñez](https://venturebeat.com/author/michael_nunez)
January 22, 2026


[![nuneybits Vector art of white goose silhouette flying through c 8100d5a7-9e36-4ed6-a188-016470e1d0e1](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F1U9H8GLIqoGqKpitVfgw3T%2Fba56292f99409eca709dac0b176ec245%2Fnuneybits_Vector_art_of_white_goose_silhouette_flying_through_c_8100d5a7-9e36-4ed6-a188-016470e1d0e1.webp%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
Credit: VentureBeat made with Midjourney](https://venturebeat.com/infrastructure/claude-code-costs-up-to-usd200-a-month-goose-does-the-same-thing-for-free)

The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it's expensive.

[Michael Nuñez](https://venturebeat.com/author/michael_nunez)
January 19, 2026


[![nuneybits Vector art of billboard with cryptic code dbe5b0ff-7644-45e6-a1ca-4a5dceeff986](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F4gD12ThOmNHZuosqC4xCTz%2F277b1e8968da602108a29fae2eaca440%2Fnuneybits_Vector_art_of_billboard_with_cryptic_code_dbe5b0ff-7644-45e6-a1ca-4a5dceeff986.webp%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
Credit: VentureBeat made with Midjourney](https://venturebeat.com/technology/listen-labs-raises-usd69m-after-viral-billboard-hiring-stunt-to-scale-ai)

Alfred Wahlforss was running out of options. His startup, [Listen Labs](https://listenlabs.ai/), needed to hire over 100 engineers, but competing against Mark Zuckerberg's [$100 million offers](https://news.bloomberglaw.com/employee-benefits/zuckerbergs-100-million-ai-job-offers-pay-off-parmy-olson) seemed impossible. So he spent $5,000 — a fifth of his marketing budget — on a [billboard in San Francisco](https://billboardinsider.com/ai-startup/) displaying what looked like gibberish: five strings of random numbers.

[Michael Nuñez](https://venturebeat.com/author/michael_nunez)
January 16, 2026


[![nuneybits Vector art of chat bubbles on a computer screen in th 5018a7ea-3496-4103-8453-7ba1b129189a](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F4Xrcg14GLKFlwSEnuEzxyS%2F21c85d29d03c4c974076475c009e3b38%2Fnuneybits_Vector_art_of_chat_bubbles_on_a_computer_screen_in_th_5018a7ea-3496-4103-8453-7ba1b129189a.webp%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
Credit: VentureBeat made with Midjourney](https://venturebeat.com/technology/salesforce-rolls-out-new-slackbot-ai-agent-as-it-battles-microsoft-and)

The new Slackbot, now generally available to [Business+](https://slack.com/pricing/businessplus) and [Enterprise+](https://slack.com/enterprise) customers, is Salesforce's most aggressive move yet to position Slack at the center of the emerging "agentic AI" movement — where software agents work alongside humans to complete complex tasks. The launch comes as Salesforce attempts to convince investors that artificial intelligence will bolster its products rather than render them obsolete.

[Michael Nuñez](https://venturebeat.com/author/michael_nunez)
January 13, 2026


[![nuneybits Hyper-realistic image of a retro computer with a glos 61ffb6e2-7c33-4d45-85f7-69c28693b3ec](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2FwHv1Wez7Ps9wYVYAo9fwT%2F14b41f606dbf1f5b17994be510407449%2Fnuneybits_Hyper-realistic_image_of_a_retro_computer_with_a_glos_61ffb6e2-7c33-4d45-85f7-69c28693b3ec.webp%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
Credit: VentureBeat made with Midjourney](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-launches-cowork-a-claude-desktop-agent-that-works-in-your-files-no)

The launch marks a major inflection point in the race to deliver practical AI agents to mainstream users, positioning Anthropic to compete not just with [OpenAI](https://openai.com/) and [Google](https://gemini.google.com/app) in conversational AI, but with [Microsoft's Copilot](https://copilot.microsoft.com/) in the burgeoning market for AI-powered productivity tools.

[Michael Nuñez](https://venturebeat.com/author/michael_nunez)
January 12, 2026


[![nuneybits Vector art of radiant skull emitting code beams deep 17d19acc-0af7-41ad-ac28-16f09ef5234b](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F66Tw6dMGGoSZZOK6XB6gm6%2F0fafc7520898e26c88edf1de9e74e863%2Fnuneybits_Vector_art_of_radiant_skull_emitting_code_beams_deep__17d19acc-0af7-41ad-ac28-16f09ef5234b.webp%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
Credit: VentureBeat made with Midjourney](https://venturebeat.com/technology/nous-researchs-nouscoder-14b-is-an-open-source-coding-model-landing-right-in)

The model, called [NousCoder-14B](https://huggingface.co/NousResearch/NousCoder-14B), is another entry in a crowded field of AI coding assistants, but arrives at a particularly charged moment: [Claude Code](https://claude.com/product/claude-code), the agentic programming tool from rival Anthropic, has dominated social media discussion since New Year's Day, with developers posting [breathless](https://x.com/0xDesigner/status/2008202211738648767?s=20) [testimonials](https://x.com/hayesdev_/status/2008043379805048948) [about its capabilities](https://x.com/0xDesigner/status/2008202211738648767?s=20). The simultaneous developments underscore how quickly AI-assisted software development is evolving — and how fiercely companies large and small are competing to capture what many believe will become a foundational technology for how software gets written.

[Michael Nuñez](https://venturebeat.com/author/michael_nunez)
January 7, 2026


[![nuneybits Vector art of programmer conducting robot orchestra i 908157a9-d44f-4bce-b390-5913b88dad27](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F6VsJWNsStTR57q9vFd5L08%2Fa0d88b4dcbd1e9ba77fd72a9c55988d9%2Fnuneybits_Vector_art_of_programmer_conducting_robot_orchestra_i_908157a9-d44f-4bce-b390-5913b88dad27.webp%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
Credit: VentureBeat made with Midjourney](https://venturebeat.com/technology/the-creator-of-claude-code-just-revealed-his-workflow-and-developers-are)

When the creator of the world's most advanced coding agent speaks, Silicon Valley doesn't just listen — it takes notes.

[Michael Nuñez](https://venturebeat.com/author/michael_nunez)
January 5, 2026


[![Wireframe silhouette guy types on laptop overlooking SF skyline shifting between day and night](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F1EELcUCWai7BHVe2Iti1Ht%2F1fef9a3dd13eeed757dfda0bdccc4403%2FlsRydt-rLj3bqzrXKMGoN_b9a12412710e4c468f59b1b66cb31312__1_.png%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
Credit: VentureBeat made with Fal.ai](https://venturebeat.com/technology/openai-debuts-gpt-5-1-codex-max-coding-model-and-it-already-completed-a-24)

OpenAI has [**introduced GPT‑5.1-Codex-Max**](https://openai.com/index/gpt-5-1-codex-max/), a new frontier agentic coding model now available in its Codex developer environment. The release marks a significant step forward in AI-assisted software engineering, offering improved long-horizon reasoning, efficiency, and real-time interactive capabilities. GPT‑5.1-Codex-Max will now replace GPT‑5.1-Codex as the default model across Codex-integrated surfaces.

[Carl Franzen](https://venturebeat.com/author/carlfranzen)
November 19, 2025


[![Benjamin Alarie (1)](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F3L8roUj3sEWN26xcxHHEHd%2F3b4214627fb0a5e6f1197c72d8436ebc%2FBenjamin_Alarie__1_.jpg%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
Benjamin Alarie, chief executive of Blue J. (Credit: Blue J)](https://venturebeat.com/technology/how-ai-tax-startup-blue-j-torched-its-entire-business-model-for-chatgpt-and)

In the winter of 2022, as the tech world was becoming mesmerized by the sudden, explosive arrival of OpenAI’s ChatGPT, [Benjamin Alarie](https://jackmanlaw.utoronto.ca/people/benjamin-alarie) faced a pivotal choice. His legal tech startup, [Blue J](https://www.bluej.com/), had a respectable business built on the AI of a bygone era, serving hundreds of law firms with predictive models. But it had hit a ceiling.

[Michael Nuñez](https://venturebeat.com/author/michael_nunez)
November 18, 2025


[![Ironwood board](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F3wiaJuUTbBrUaBp8cSXtO4%2F81095c7817da6a2967a961ed60356ed4%2FIronwood_board.jpg%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)\\
\\
Image Credit: Google](https://venturebeat.com/infrastructure/google-debuts-ai-chips-with-4x-performance-boost-secures-anthropic-megadeal)

The announcement, made Thursday, centers on [Ironwood](https://cloud.google.com/resources/ironwood-tpu-interest?utm_source=cgc-blog&utm_medium=blog&utm_campaign=FY25-Q2-global-ENT33820-website-cs-ironwood-tpu-interest&utm_content=ironwood_announcement_blog&utm_term=ironwood&hl=en), Google's latest custom AI accelerator chip, which will become generally available in the coming weeks. In a striking validation of the technology, [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/), the AI safety company behind the Claude family of models, disclosed plans to access up to [one million of these TPU chips](https://www.googlecloudpresscorner.com/2025-10-23-Anthropic-to-Expand-Use-of-Google-Cloud-TPUs-and-Services) — a commitment worth tens of billions of dollars and among the largest known AI infrastructure deals to date.

[Michael Nuñez](https://venturebeat.com/author/michael_nunez)
November 6, 2025


[![Credit: VentureBeat made with Midjourney](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F2buVKIMlQ2KFcFg8BPHoqU%2Fc5d2dfbfd8cfe9a9432106a936fa204f%2Fnuneybits_A_retro_glowing_computer_on_gradient_background_that__094dfc70-9906-4074-bb00-d32b04faf5f9-1.webp%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)](https://venturebeat.com/infrastructure/the-most-important-openai-announcement-you-probably-missed-at-devday-2025)

OpenAI’s annual developer conference on Monday was a spectacle of ambitious AI product launches, from an [app store for ChatGPT](https://openai.com/index/introducing-apps-in-chatgpt/) to a stunning [video-generation API](https://openai.com/index/sora-2/) that brought creative concepts to life. But for the enterprises and technical leaders watching closely, the most consequential announcement was the quiet [general availability of Codex](https://openai.com/index/codex-now-generally-available/), the company's AI software engineer. This release signals a profound shift in how software—and by extension, modern business—is built.

[Michael Nuñez](https://venturebeat.com/author/michael_nunez)
October 9, 2025


[![Credit: VentureBeat made with Midjourney](https://venturebeat.com/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fimages.ctfassets.net%2Fjdtwqhzvc2n1%2F5SsXpDqbiyZvm7DJbht3pv%2F765b6ae01f75c4e84f15e6f9f026bdf2%2Fnuneybits_A_developer_working_on_a_retro_computer_on_gradient_b_a06360d7-aa20-4d82-949f-48730f690988.webp%3Fw%3D1000%26q%3D100&w=3840&q=50)](https://venturebeat.com/technology/openai-dev-day-2025-chatgpt-becomes-the-new-app-store-and-hardware-is-coming)

In a packed hall at Fort Mason Center in San Francisco, against a backdrop of the Golden Gate Bridge, [OpenAI](https://openai.com/) CEO Sam Altman laid out a bold vision to remake the digital world. The company that brought generative AI to the mainstream with a simple chatbot is now building the foundations for its next act: a comprehensive computing platform designed to move beyond the screen and browser, with legendary designer Jony Ive enlisted to help shape its physical form.

[Michael Nuñez](https://venturebeat.com/author/michael_nunez)
October 7, 2025
